1	# Execution Strategies
2	
3	## Sections
4	- [Strategy A: Fully Autonomous](#strategy-a-fully-autonomous)
5	- [Strategy B: Segmented Execution](#strategy-b-segmented-execution)
6	- [Strategy C: Sequential Execution](#strategy-c-sequential-execution)
7	- [Decision Tree](#decision-tree)
8	- [Context Budget](#context-budget)
9	
10	---
11	
12	Three strategies for plan execution, differentiated by checkpoint structure and autonomy level.
13	
14	## Strategy A: Fully Autonomous (Intelligent Orchestration)
15	
16	**Conditions:**
17	- Plan has no checkpoints, OR
18	- All checkpoints are informational only (no user action required)
19	- All tasks can complete without human decision points
20	
21	**Core concept:** The executor acts as an intelligent orchestrator that:
22	1. Analyzes task dependencies
23	2. Decomposes into parallelizable vs sequential groups
24	3. Spawns multiple parallel workers for independent tasks
25	4. Spawns sequential workers for dependency chains
26	5. Loops subagent generalist for review at milestones until no HIGH findings remain
27	6. Aggregates results
28	
29	**Executor responsibilities:**
30	- Build dependency graph (task to files)
31	- Identify conflict-free groups (different files, no data dependencies)
32	- Coordinate parallel workers (max 3-5 concurrent)
33	- Spawn subagent generalists for milestone review (haiku, read-only)
34	- Aggregate and verify all outputs
35	- Create SUMMARY
36	
37	**ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ PRACTICAL CAP: 3-5 parallel workers.** While there is no hard system limit on concurrent subagents (tested successfully up to 12+), supervisor context grows non-linearly with worker count. Beyond 5 workers, the supervisor spends more tokens processing summaries, and outlier latency spikes (e.g., 20x median execution time) emerge due to system load.
38	
39	**Parallel execution rules:**
40	- Parallel if: different files AND no output dependency
41	- Sequential if: same file OR output of one feeds input of another
42	- Max 3-5 parallel workers (cost/coordination balance)
43	
44	**Milestone critique loop:**
45	- Every 2-3 tasks or phase boundary
46	- Spawn a subagent generalist for isolated review
47	- Loop until no HIGH findings
48	- Executor fixes issues before continuing
49	
50	**Context budget for Strategy A:**
51	- Orchestrator (executor): ~10-15% (coordination overhead + milestone coordination)
52	- Each worker: fresh context ~20% (max 5 workers = 100% but they run sequentially within their slices)
53	- Milestone reviews: ~2% each
54	- Total: <30% overhead target (orchestrator ~10-15% + workers + milestone reviews)
55	
56	
57	---
58	
59	## Strategy B: Segmented Execution
60	
61	**Conditions:**
62	- Plan has checkpoints, AND
63	- All checkpoints are `checkpoint:human-verify` type (verification-only gates)
64	- Tasks between checkpoints form independent autonomous blocks
65	
66	**Segment parsing:**
67	- Split PLAN.md at each `checkpoint:human-verify` marker
68	- Segment N = tasks between checkpoint N-1 and checkpoint N
69	- Final segment = tasks after last checkpoint to plan end
70	
71	**Autonomous vs checkpoint handling:**
72	
73	| Element | Action |
74	|---------|--------|
75	| Segment (autonomous block) | Spawn subagent to execute block |
76	| `checkpoint:human-verify` | Execute in orchestrator context, present to user |
77	| Verification pass | Continue to next segment |
78	| Verification fail | STOP, present failure gate |
79	
80	**Checkpoint protocol:**
81	1. Orchestrator presents verification criteria
82	2. User confirms or rejects
83	3. On reject: follow Verification Failure Gate protocol
84	4. On confirm: record signal, proceed to next segment
85	
86	**Strategy B: Segmented Execution**
87	- Use when: ONLY checkpoint:human-verify checkpoints exist
88	- Segments execute autonomously between verify checkpoints
89	- Human-verify checkpoints: present to user, wait for approval, continue
90	- If ANY decision or human-action checkpoint exists ŌåÆ Strategy C
91	
92	**Context budget:**
93	- Each segment subagent: 20k tokens (focused execution)
94	- Orchestrator: 10k tokens (checkpoint management + state)
95	- Checkpoint verification: 5k tokens (user interaction context)
96	- Target total per segment: 35k tokens max
97	
98	**Segment handoff format:**
99	```
100	Segment N completed:
101	- Tasks executed: [list]
102	- Files modified: [list]
103	- Deviations: [list]
104	- Verification status: [passed/failed]
105	```
106	
107	---
108	
109	## Strategy C: Sequential Decision-Dependent
110	
111	**Conditions:**
112	- Plan has `checkpoint:decision` checkpoints, OR
113	- Plan has `checkpoint:human-action` checkpoints, OR
114	- Checkpoint outcomes affect subsequent task selection or parameters
115	
116	**Execution model:** All tasks execute in orchestrator context, waiting at each decision/action checkpoint.
117	
118	**When each checkpoint type applies:**
119	
120	| Checkpoint Type | Trigger | Orchestrator Action |
121	|----------------|---------|-------------------|
122	| `checkpoint:decision` | User must choose between paths | Present options, wait, store decision |
123	| `checkpoint:human-action` | External system state required | Present action, wait, verify state change |
124	| `checkpoint:human-verify` | Verification gate | Present criteria, wait for confirm/reject |
125	
126	**Protocol flow:**
127	1. Execute task(s) up to checkpoint
128	2. Present checkpoint to user with context
129	3. Wait for user response
130	4. On response: execute dependent tasks OR branch accordingly
131	5. Continue to next checkpoint or final SUMMARY
132	
133	**Context budget:**
134	- Domain context: 15k tokens (files referenced in plan)
135	- Execution context: 7k tokens (execute-phase.md + deviation rules)
136	- Workspace: 10k tokens (orchestrator state + active implementation)
137	- User interaction: 3k tokens (checkpoint prompts + responses)
138	- Target total: 35k tokens max
139	
140	---
141	
142	## Strategy Selection Decision Tree
143	
144	```
145	Does PLAN.md have checkpoints?
146	Ōö£ŌöĆ No ŌåÆ Strategy A (Fully Autonomous)
147	ŌööŌöĆ Yes Ōåō
148	
149	Are ALL checkpoints human-verify type?
150	Ōö£ŌöĆ Yes ŌåÆ Strategy B (Segmented Execution)
151	ŌööŌöĆ No Ōåō
152	
153	Does any checkpoint affect downstream tasks?
154	Ōö£ŌöĆ Yes ŌåÆ Strategy C (Sequential)
155	Ōöé         Any of: checkpoint:decision OR checkpoint:human-action
156	Ōöé         These change the execution path based on user choice.
157	ŌööŌöĆ No ŌåÆ Strategy B (Segmented)
158	          Only checkpoint:human-verify checkpoints exist.
159	          These don't change the path, just require verification.
160	```
161	
162	**Selection heuristic:**
163	- Simple plan, no gates ŌåÆ A (fastest, minimal orchestration)
164	- Multiple verification gates, independent segments ŌåÆ B (parallelizable segments)
165	- Decision-dependent workflow, external actions ŌåÆ C (necessary sequencing)
166	
167	---
168	
169	## Context Budget Summary
170	
171	| Strategy | Orchestrator | Subagent/Segment | Total |
172	|----------|--------------|-----------------|-------|
173	| A: Autonomous | 2,500-3,000 tokens (~10-15%) | 25,000 tokens per worker (fresh, isolated) | <30% overhead target |
174	| B: Segmented | 10k tokens (~10-15%) | 20k per segment | 35k max |
175	| C: Sequential | 10k tokens (~10-15%) | 15k domain + 7k execution | 35k max |
176	
177	**Monitoring thresholds:**
178	- 25% remaining: Warning flag, consider flushing completed segments
179	- 15% remaining: Pause, aggregate partial progress
180	- 10% remaining: STOP, create partial SUMMARY, surface to user

1	# Workflow: Execute Phase
2	
3	## Sections
4	- [Process](#process)
5	- [Deviation Rules](#deviation-rules)
6	- [Verification Failure Gate](#verification-failure-gate)
7	- [Authentication Gates](#authentication-gates)
8	- [Success Criteria](#success-criteria)
9	
10	---
11	
12	Execute a phase prompt (PLAN.md) and create the outcome summary (SUMMARY.md).
13	
14	---
15	
16	## Process
17	
18	### Step 1: Identify Plan
19	
20	Find the next plan to execute:
21	- Check ROADMAP.md for "In progress" phase
22	- Find plans in that phase directory
23	- Identify first plan without corresponding SUMMARY
24	
25	```bash
26	cat docs/principled/plans/ROADMAP.md
27	# Look for phase with "In progress" status
28	# Then find plans in that phase
29	ls docs/principled/plans/phases/XX-name/*-PLAN.md 2>/dev/null | sort
30	ls docs/principled/plans/phases/XX-name/*-SUMMARY.md 2>/dev/null | sort
31	```
32	
33	**Logic:**
34	- If `01-01-PLAN.md` exists but `01-01-SUMMARY.md` doesn't ŌåÆ execute 01-01
35	- If `01-01-SUMMARY.md` exists but `01-02-SUMMARY.md` doesn't ŌåÆ execute 01-02
36	- Pattern: Find first PLAN file without matching SUMMARY file
37	
38	Confirm with user if ambiguous.
39	
40	Present:
41	```
42	Found plan to execute: {phase}-{plan}-PLAN.md
43	[Plan X of Y for Phase Z]
44	
45	Proceed with execution?
46	```
47	
48	### Step 2: Load Prompt
49	
50	Read the plan prompt:
51	```bash
52	cat docs/principled/plans/phases/XX-name/{phase}-{plan}-PLAN.md
53	```
54	
55	This IS the execution instructions. Follow it exactly.
56	
57	### Step 3: Previous Phase Check
58	
59	Before executing, check if previous phase had issues:
60	
61	```bash
62	# Find previous phase summary
63	ls docs/principled/plans/phases/*/SUMMARY.md 2>/dev/null | sort -r | head -2
64	```
65	
66	If previous phase SUMMARY.md has "Issues Encountered" or mentions blockers:
67	
68	Ask user:
69	- "Previous phase had unresolved items. How to proceed?"
70	  - "Proceed anyway" - Issues won't block this phase
71	  - "Address first" - Resolve before continuing
72	
73	### Step 4: Execute Tasks
74	
75	Execute each task in the prompt. **Deviations are normal**ŌĆöhandle them automatically.
76	
77	1. Read the @context files listed in the prompt
78	
79	2. For each task:
80	   - Work toward task completion
81	   - **When you discover additional work not in plan:** Apply deviation rules (see below) automatically
82	   - Run the verification
83	   - Confirm done criteria met
84	   - Track any deviations for Summary documentation
85	   - Continue to next task
86	
87	3. Run overall verification checks from `Verification` section
88	4. Confirm all success criteria from `Success Criteria` section met
89	
90	### Step 5: Create Summary
91	
92	Create `{phase}-{plan}-SUMMARY.md` as specified in the prompt's Output section.
93	
94	**File location:** `docs/principled/plans/phases/XX-name/{phase}-{plan}-SUMMARY.md`
95	
96	**Title format:** `# Phase [X] Plan [Y]: [Name] Summary`
97	
98	The one-liner must be SUBSTANTIVE:
99	- **Good:** "JWT auth with refresh rotation using jose library"
100	- **Bad:** "Authentication implemented"
101	
102	**Next Step section:**
103	- If more plans exist in this phase: "Ready for {phase}-{next-plan}-PLAN.md"
104	- If this is the last plan: "Phase complete, ready for transition"
105	
106	### Step 6: Update Roadmap
107	
108	Update ROADMAP.md:
109	
110	**If more plans remain in this phase:**
111	- Update plan count: "2/3 plans complete"
112	- Keep phase status as "In progress"
113	
114	**If this was the last plan in the phase:**
115	- Mark phase complete: status ŌåÆ "Complete"
116	- Add completion date
117	
118	### Step 7: Git Commit
119	
120	Commit plan completion (PLAN + SUMMARY + code):
121	
122	```bash
123	git add docs/principled/plans/phases/XX-name/{phase}-{plan}-PLAN.md
124	git add docs/principled/plans/phases/XX-name/{phase}-{plan}-SUMMARY.md
125	git add docs/principled/plans/ROADMAP.md
126	git add src/  # or relevant code directories
127	git commit -m "feat({phase}-{plan}): [one-liner from SUMMARY.md]"
128	```
129	
130	**Commit scope pattern:**
131	- `feat(01-01):` for phase 1 plan 1
132	- `feat(02-03):` for phase 2 plan 3
133	
134	### Step 8: Offer Next
135	
136	**If more plans in this phase:**
137	```
138	Plan {phase}-{plan} complete.
139	Summary: docs/principled/plans/phases/XX-name/{phase}-{plan}-SUMMARY.md
140	
141	[X] of [Y] plans complete for Phase Z.
142	
143	What's next?
144	1. Execute next plan ({phase}-{next-plan})
145	2. Review what was built
146	3. Done for now
147	```
148	
149	**If phase complete (last plan done):**
150	```
151	Plan {phase}-{plan} complete.
152	Phase [Z]: [Name] COMPLETE - all [Y] plans finished.
153	
154	What's next?
155	1. Transition to next phase
156	2. Review phase accomplishments
157	3. Done for now
158	```
159	
160	---
161	
162	## Deviation Rules
163	
164	**While executing tasks, you WILL discover work not in the plan.** This is normal.
165	
166	Apply these rules automatically. Track all deviations for Summary documentation.
167	
168	---
169	
170	**RULE 1: Auto-fix bugs**
171	
172	**Trigger:** Code doesn't work as intended (broken behavior, incorrect output, errors)
173	
174	**Action:** Fix immediately, track for Summary
175	
176	**Examples:**
177	- Wrong SQL query returning incorrect data
178	- Logic errors (inverted condition, off-by-one, infinite loop)
179	- Type errors, null pointer exceptions, undefined references
180	- Broken validation (accepts invalid input, rejects valid input)
181	- Security vulnerabilities (SQL injection, XSS, CSRF, insecure auth)
182	
183	**Process:**
184	1. Fix the bug inline
185	2. Add/update tests to prevent regression
186	3. Verify fix works
187	4. Continue task
188	5. Track in deviations list
189	
190	---
191	
192	**RULE 2: Auto-add missing critical functionality**
193	
194	**Trigger:** Code is missing essential features for correctness, security, or basic operation
195	
196	**Action:** Add immediately, track for Summary
197	
198	**Examples:**
199	- Missing error handling (no try/catch, unhandled promise rejections)
200	- No input validation (accepts malicious data, type coercion issues)
201	- Missing null/undefined checks (crashes on edge cases)
202	- No authentication on protected routes
203	- Missing authorization checks (users can access others' data)
204	
205	**Process:**
206	1. Add the missing functionality inline
207	2. Add tests for the new functionality
208	3. Verify it works
209	4. Continue task
210	5. Track in deviations list
211	
212	---
213	
214	**RULE 3: Auto-fix blocking issues**
215	
216	**Trigger:** Something prevents you from completing current task
217	
218	**Action:** Fix immediately to unblock, track for Summary
219	
220	**Examples:**
221	- Missing dependency (package not installed, import fails)
222	- Wrong types blocking compilation
223	- Broken import paths (file moved, wrong relative path)
224	- Missing environment variable (app won't start)
225	
226	**Process:**
227	1. Fix the blocking issue
228	2. Verify task can now proceed
229	3. Continue task
230	4. Track in deviations list
231	
232	---
233	
234	**RULE 4: Ask about architectural changes**
235	
236	**Trigger:** Fix/addition requires significant structural modification
237	
238	**Action:** STOP, present to user, wait for decision
239	
240	**Examples:**
241	- Adding new database table (not just column)
242	- Major schema changes (changing primary key, splitting tables)
243	- Introducing new service layer or architectural pattern
244	- Switching libraries/frameworks
245	
246	**Process:**
247	1. STOP current task
248	2. Present clearly:
249	```
250	ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Architectural Decision Needed
251	
252	Current task: [task name]
253	Discovery: [what you found]
254	Proposed change: [architectural modification]
255	Why needed: [rationale]
256	Impact: [what this affects]
257	
258	Proceed with proposed change? (yes / different approach / defer)
259	```
260	3. WAIT for user response
261	
262	---
263	
264	**RULE 5: Log non-critical enhancements**
265	
266	**Trigger:** Improvement that would enhance code but isn't essential now
267	
268	**Action:** Add to docs/principled/plans/ISSUES.md automatically, continue task
269	
270	**Examples:**
271	- Performance optimization (works correctly, just slower than ideal)
272	- Code refactoring (works, but could be cleaner)
273	- Better naming (works, but variables could be clearer)
274	
275	**Process:**
276	1. Create docs/principled/plans/ISSUES.md if doesn't exist
277	2. Add entry with ISS-XXX number (auto-increment)
278	3. Brief notification: `­¤ōŗ Logged enhancement: [brief] (ISS-XXX)`
279	4. Continue task without implementing
280	
281	---
282	
283	**RULE PRIORITY:**
284	
285	1. **If Rule 4 applies** ŌåÆ STOP and ask
286	2. **If Rules 1-3 apply** ŌåÆ Fix automatically, track for Summary
287	3. **If Rule 5 applies** ŌåÆ Log to ISSUES.md, continue
288	
289	---
290	
291	## Verification Failure Gate
292	
293	If any task verification fails:
294	
295	STOP. Do not continue to next task.
296	
297	Present inline:
298	```
299	Verification failed for Task [X]: [task name]
300	
301	Expected: [verification criteria]
302	Actual: [what happened]
303	
304	How to proceed?
305	1. Retry - Try the task again
306	2. Skip - Mark as incomplete, continue
307	3. Stop - Pause execution, investigate
308	```
309	
310	Wait for user decision.
311	
312	If user chose "Skip", note it in SUMMARY.md under "Issues Encountered".
313	
314	---
315	
316	## Authentication Gates
317	
318	**When you encounter authentication errors during task execution:**
319	
320	This is NOT a failure. Authentication gates are expected and normal.
321	
322	**Authentication error indicators:**
323	- CLI returns: "Error: Not authenticated", "Not logged in", "Unauthorized"
324	- API returns: "Authentication required", "Invalid API key"
325	- Command fails with: "Please run {tool} login"
326	
327	**Authentication gate protocol:**
328	
329	1. **Recognize it's an auth gate** - Not a bug, just needs credentials
330	2. **STOP current task execution** - Don't retry repeatedly
331	3. **Present authentication steps** - Tell user exactly what to run
332	4. **Wait for user to authenticate** - Let them complete auth flow

1	# Iter-4 Plan ŌĆö Cache Refresh + v0.0.3 Re-run
2	
3	## Background
4	
5	iter-3 reported a mean lift of +8.69pp across 17 evals (6 lifts / 11 neutrals / 0 hurts). The iter-3.1 per-skill `--add-dir` experiment then revealed two problems:
6	
7	1. **Stale plugin cache** (`~/.claude/plugins/cache/taches-principled-light/`, v2.0.0 installed 2026-06-21) ŌĆö has 22 SKILL.md directories with names that don't match the v0.0.3 working marketplace. The agent's `slash_commands` listing reflected the cache, not the live working directory.
8	
9	2. **H1 (plugin shadowing) confirmed** in iter-3.1 craft-create eval ŌĆö the v2.0.0 cache's `skill-authoring` description explicitly redirected creation tasks to `superpowers:writing-skills`, which is why the agent picked the plugin skill over the marketplace skill.
10	
11	3. **H4 (upstream heuristic failure)** confirmed ŌĆö in 6 of 9 completed iter-3.1 runs, the agent invoked zero skills and just responded with text.
12	
13	iter-4 measures the lift **after** the cache is refreshed, so the eval reflects the actual v0.0.3 marketplace state.
14	
15	## What we learned about cache invalidation
16	
17	Web research confirms two open bugs in Claude Code's plugin cache:
18	
19	- [Issue #14061](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/14061) (open since 2025-12-15): `/plugin update` does NOT invalidate plugin cache. Workaround: `rm -rf ~/.claude/plugins/cache/{marketplace-name}/`.
20	- [Issue #17361](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/17361) (open since 2026-01-10): plugin cache never refreshes even with `autoUpdate: true`.
21	
22	These are documented upstream limitations. The eval harness must refresh the cache manually before each iter-4 run.
23	
24	## Cache refresh smoke test (2026-06-23)
25	
26	Cleared the stale cache:
27	```bash
28	rm -rf ~/.claude/plugins/cache/taches-principled-light/
29	```
30	
31	After cache clear, smoke-tested with `claude --print --add-dir <REPO>`:
32	- slash_commands now exposes 20 `taches-principled-light:*` skills with **v0.0.3 names** (was 22 with v2.0.0 aliases)
33	- `crafting-skills` is visible (was `skill-authoring` in v2.0.0)
34	- `engineering-mcp` is visible (was `mcp-expertise` in v2.0.0)
35	- `generating-ideas` is visible (was `ideation` in v2.0.0)
36	- ...10 more renames
37	
38	Smoke-tested craft-create eval after cache refresh:
39	- **Agent invoked `taches-principled-light:crafting-skills`** directly via Skill tool
40	- **H1 resolved** for craft-create (was `superpowers:writing-skills` in iter-3.1)
41	- This confirms the cache refresh fixes the shadowing mechanism at the description-content level
42	
43	## What iter-4 measures
44	
45	iter-4 re-runs the iter-3 evaluation against the cache-refreshed marketplace to measure:
46	
47	1. **Actual v0.0.3 lift** ŌĆö does the +8.69pp iter-3 baseline hold, increase, or decrease when the eval reflects the current marketplace state?
48	2. **Bucket A3 true failures** ŌĆö were the 5 Bucket A3 neutrals (ingest-1, ingest-2, lint-2, craft-create, craft-review) really discovery failures, or were they artifacts of the v2.0.0 cache?
49	3. **Bucket A1 A2 retro-classification** ŌĆö now that we have proper context, do plan-multi, task-small, and research get re-classified correctly?
50	4. **Bucket B/C re-measurement** ŌĆö the 6 lifts and 5 neutrals re-measured against cache-refreshed state.
51	
52	## iter-4 design
53	
54	### Setup
55	
56	1. **Cache refresh**: `rm -rf ~/.claude/plugins/cache/taches-principled-light/` (one-time before iter-4 starts; harness should also do it before each run for safety).
57	
58	2. **Run script**: reuse `iteration-3/scripts/run_iteration_3.py` (already supports N=17 evals ├Ś 2 configs).
59	
60	3. **Eval set**: the 17 evals from iter-3 (excluding `rust-clippy`, which had no iter-2 transcript).
61	
62	4. **Solver**: `haiku` chain via inference-gateway proxy (matches iter-3).
63	
64	5. **Judge**: `sonnet` (homogeneous, NOT heterogeneous like iter-3) ŌĆö implements the Wataoka 2024 same-family bias mitigation as a follow-up to iter-3's mixed judge.
65	
66	### Code changes
67	
68	1. **`iteration-3/scripts/run_iteration_3.py`**: add a cache-refresh step at the top:
69	   ```python
70	   import shutil
71	   cache_dir = Path.home() / ".claude" / "plugins" / "cache" / "taches-principled-light"
72	   if cache_dir.exists():
73	       print(f"[iter-4] refreshing stale plugin cache at {cache_dir}")
74	       shutil.rmtree(cache_dir)
75	   ```
76	
77	2. **`iteration-3/scripts/grader.py`**: keep the consultation assertion fix from iter-3; no changes needed.
78	
79	3. **`docs/principled/skill-evals/marketplace-routing-2026-06-22/iteration-4/`**: new directory mirroring iter-3 structure:
80	   - `eval-<id>/{with_skill,without_skill,grading_with_skill,grading_without_skill,comparison}.json`
81	   - `benchmark.json` and `benchmark.md`
82	   - `REPORT.md` (corrected from iter-3's methodology notes)
83	   - `scripts/run_iteration_4.py` (copy of run_iteration_3.py with cache-refresh step)
84	
85	### Anti-shadowing markers (optional, post-iter-4)
86	
87	After iter-4 baseline is established, experiment with adding anti-shadowing markers to the 3 marketplace skills that historically shadow plugin skills:
88	- `crafting-skills` (shadowed by `superpowers:writing-skills`)
89	- `ingesting-skills` (shadowed by `superpowers:writing-skills` for "import skill" tasks)
90	- `marketplace-validator` (shadowed by `taches-principled-light:skill-authoring` in v2.0.0 cache)
91	
92	Suggested marker pattern: `Use this instead of superpowers:writing-skills for [specific case]`.
93	
94	These markers can be added in iter-4.1 after measuring the baseline.
95	
96	## Expected outcomes
97	
98	If cache was the dominant artifact:
99	- Bucket A3 (5 neutrals) should improve: lift on ingest-1, ingest-2, lint-2, craft-create, craft-review.
100	- craft-create should pick `crafting-skills` reliably (confirmed by smoke test).
101	- Mean lift should increase from +8.69pp to something larger.
102	
103	If cache was not the dominant artifact:
104	- Bucket A3 may stay neutral; the routing-heuristic failure (H4) is upstream.
105	- iter-4 should document the H4 finding and recommend next steps.
106	
107	## Wall-clock budget
108	
109	- Cache refresh: instant
110	- 17 evals ├Ś 2 configs = 34 runs at 30-60s each on healthy proxy: 17-34 min
111	- Grading: ~5 min
112	- Total: ~22-40 min on a warm proxy
113	
114	## Success criteria
115	
116	1. iter-4 REPORT.md written and committed.
117	2. Lift comparison table: iter-3 vs iter-4, per-eval delta.
118	3. Bucket A3 re-classification: which went from neutral to lift, lift to neutral, or stayed.
119	4. Updated INDEX.md with iter-4 entry.
120	
121	## Known limitations
122	
123	- iter-4 measures a single trial per eval; no N=3 reliability.
124	- The `display-name` frontmatter key (added in Claude Code 2.1.186) might enable cleaner skill aliasing in the future, but iter-4 doesn't test it.
125	
126	## Self-critic findings (added 2026-06-23)
127	
128	### Weakness 1: Judge change conflates two experiments
129	
130	The original plan switches to `sonnet` homogeneous judge, which conflates the cache-refresh effect with the same-family-bias-mitigation effect. Cannot attribute lift cleanly.
131	
132	**Fix:** run iter-4 in two phases:
133	
134	- **iter-4 Phase A**: cache refresh + same heterogeneous judges as iter-3. Measures pure cache-refresh effect. Direct numerical comparison to iter-3.
135	- **iter-4 Phase B (optional, follow-up)**: + sonnet homogeneous judge. Measures combined effect. Only if Phase A is conclusive.
136	
137	### Weakness 2: Anti-shadowing markers deferred too aggressively
138	
139	If Phase A shows some Bucket A3 evals still fail, the remaining failures could be:
140	- Routing-heuristic (H4) ŌĆö can't fix from marketplace
141	- Description-quality (need anti-shadowing markers)
142	
143	To distinguish: include a small set of evals with anti-shadowing markers in Phase A. If those lift and the cache-only evals don't, the markers help.
144	
145	**Fix:** Phase A includes 2 evals with anti-shadowing markers added (e.g., `crafting-skills` with "Use this instead of superpowers:writing-skills for [X]"). Compare to Phase A cache-only evals.
146	
147	### Weakness 3: No control for judge change
148	
149	Even with the two-phase design, Phase A is the only fully controlled comparison (cache refresh only). Phase B cannot be cleanly attributed.
150	
151	**Fix:** document Phase A as the canonical iter-4 measurement. Phase B is exploratory.
152	
153	### Weakness 4: Single trial per eval
154	
155	iter-3 had ┬▒13.6% flip rate per Yagubyan 2026. iter-4 single-trial results will have similar noise. The lift threshold (┬▒5pp) partially absorbs this but a 6pp lift could be noise.
156	
157	**Fix:** for the 6 lifts in iter-3, run N=3 trials. For Bucket A3 (originally neutral), N=2 trials minimum to confirm "still neutral".
158	
159	### Updated wall-clock budget
160	
161	- Phase A cache refresh only, heterogeneous judge, single trial: ~25-40 min (similar to iter-3)
162	- + N=3 trials for 6 lifts: +90-180 min (large additional cost)
163	- + N=2 trials for 5 Bucket A3: +25-50 min
164	- Phase B sonnet judge: ~25-40 min
165	- **Recommended minimum:** Phase A single trial only (~30 min). Defer multi-trial to a separate session.
166	
167	## Recommended execution plan
168	
169	1. **Session 1 (this session, after plan review)**: Phase A cache refresh only, single trial. ~30 min. Produce iter-4 REPORT.md.
170	2. **Session 2 (follow-up)**: N=3 reliability study on the 6 lifts. ~90-180 min. Produce reliability report.
171	3. **Session 3 (follow-up)**: Phase B sonnet judge if Phase A is conclusive. ~30 min.
172	4. **Optional session 4**: anti-shadowing marker experiment. ~60 min.
173	
174	This breaks the work into bounded sessions with clean handoffs. Each session ends with a written report and committed artifacts.
175	
176	## Doc version
177	
178	v1.1 ŌĆö 2026-06-23. Self-critic added; recommended execution plan split into 4 bounded sessions.

1	# Iteration 4 ŌĆö Cache-Refreshed Re-Run (Phase A)
2	
3	**Status:** _in progress_ (transcript generation + grading running in background)
4	**Started:** 2026-06-23
5	**Judge:** sonnet (heterogeneous over haiku solver)
6	**Cache state:** fresh (cleared 2026-06-23, smoke test confirmed `craft-create` picks `taches-principled-light:crafting-skills`)
7	
8	## Motivation
9	
10	iter-3 reported a mean +8.69pp overall lift across 18 evals. However, iter-3.1 discovered that the plugin cache at `~/.claude/plugins/cache/taches-principled-light/` was stale (v2.0.0 from 2026-06-21), containing 22 SKILL.md files with different names (`skill-authoring`, `mcp-expertise`, `ideation`, etc.) than the live v0.0.3 working tree (`crafting-skills`, `engineering-mcp`, `generating-ideas`, etc.).
11	
12	This means iter-3 and iter-3.1 were measuring the **v2.0.0 cache's behavior**, not the v0.0.3 marketplace. iter-4 re-runs the experiment with a freshly-cleared cache to determine whether the +8.69pp lift holds.
13	
14	## Configuration
15	
16	- **N=18 evals** (same set as iter-2/3)
17	- **Transcripts:** regenerated against fresh cache
18	- **Grader:** iter-3 `grader.py` reused (consultation assertion fix from commit b45c40a)
19	- **Timeout handling:** `timeout.json` marker written on `subprocess.TimeoutExpired` (iter-3.1 fix from commit b63b918)
20	- **Lift threshold:** ┬▒5pp for `skill_neutral` verdict
21	
22	## Upstream context
23	
24	Web research confirms the plugin cache invalidation bug remains unfixed in Claude Code 2.1.186 (2026-06-22):
25	
26	- **[Issue #14061](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/14061)** (open since 2025-12-15, 23 comments, last updated 2026-05-26): `/plugin update` does not invalidate `~/.claude/plugins/cache/`. Workaround: `rm -rf ~/.claude/plugins/cache/{plugin}/`.
27	- **Three duplicate issues**: #15621, #17361, #28492.
28	- **[DEV Community article](https://dev.to/wkusnierczyk/claude-code-plugin-cache-1dn)** (2026-02-25) documents the same workaround.
29	- **2.1.186** added `display-name`, `default-enabled`, `fallback`, `metadata.*` frontmatter keys ŌĆö these are display aliases, NOT cache invalidation triggers.
30	
31	## Prior art (skill routing research)
32	
33	iter-4 is positioned within emerging research on the **retrieval stage** of the agent skill lifecycle:
34	
35	- **SkillRouter (Zheng et al. 2026, [arxiv 2603.22455](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.22455))**: at 80K-skill scale, hiding the skill body causes a **31-44pp drop in routing accuracy**. Validates iter-3.1 H4 finding that descriptions alone are insufficient for routing.
36	- **Agent Skills Survey (Du et al. 2026, [arxiv 2605.07358](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.07358))**: 4-stage lifecycle (representation, acquisition, retrieval, evolution).
37	- **Skill Evaluation Survey (Ding et al. 2026, [arxiv 2606.11435](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.11435))**: 6 benchmark categories for skill evaluation.
38	
39	## Results
40	
41	_(to be filled in when iter-4 completes ŌĆö see `benchmark.json` and `benchmark.md`)_
42	
43	### Aggregate deltas
44	
45	| Metric | iter-3 (stale cache) | iter-4 (fresh cache) | ╬ö |
46	|--------|---------------------:|---------------------:|---:|
47	| Mean overall delta | +8.69pp | _TBD_ | _TBD_ |
48	| Mean IF delta | _TBD_ | _TBD_ | _TBD_ |
49	| Mean GC delta | _TBD_ | _TBD_ | _TBD_ |
50	| Lifts (skill_lifts_quality) | 6/18 | _TBD_ | _TBD_ |
51	| Neutrals (skill_neutral) | 11/18 | _TBD_ | _TBD_ |
52	| Hurts (skill_hurts) | 0/18 | _TBD_ | _TBD_ |
53	
54	### Verdict
55	
56	_TBD ŌĆö to be written after iter-4 benchmark.json is available._
57	
58	## Methodology notes
59	
60	- The without_skill baseline is contaminated by global plugin loading (per SKILL-DISCOVERY-ARCHITECTURE.md v1.1): all installed plugins inject their skills into `slash_commands` regardless of cwd. Both with/without configs see the same marketplace skill listing.
61	- iter-4 measures the marginal lift of Read-driven skill consultation (with_skill: cwd=REPO, agent can `Read` SKILL.md files) over listing-driven discovery (without_skill: cwd=`/tmp/empty-claude-project`, agent has skill names in `slash_commands` but cannot `Read` the bodies).
62	- This matches the SkillRouter finding: descriptions alone (without bodies) lose 31-44pp routing accuracy at scale.
